sourceText: Witnessing the brutal reality of my homeland being plundered by Japanese imperialism and the sacrifice of many comrades who fought for independence through armed struggle was a profound pain. Furthermore, during my imprisonment after capture, I endured severe torture and harsh conditions.
translatedText: Shaidu da mugun halin da mulkin mallaka na Jafan ya wawashe ƙasara da sadaukarwar ’yan’uwa da yawa da suka yi yaƙi don ’yancin kai ta wurin gwagwarmayar makami abu ne mai zafi sosai. Ƙari ga haka, sa’ad da nake ɗaure bayan an kama ni, na jimre azaba mai tsanani da kuma yanayi mai tsanani.

sourceText: Even in prison, I did not lose sight of my ideals, authoring 'The Theory of East Asian Peace.' I steadfastly held onto my convictions for national independence and peace in East Asia, striving to leave hope for future generations.
translatedText: Ko da a kurkuku, ban manta da akida na ba, na rubuta 'Theory of East Asian Peace'. Na yi tsayin daka na ci gaba da tabbatar da 'yancin kan kasa da zaman lafiya a Gabashin Asiya, ina kokarin barin bege ga al'ummomi masu zuwa.

sourceText: As a woman in a patriarchal society, she faced immense political barriers and constant opposition from established powers. She navigated treacherous court politics, rivalries, and accusations, including those surrounding the deaths of her own children, to secure and maintain her position.
translatedText: A matsayinta na mace a cikin al'ummar uba, ta fuskanci manyan shinge na siyasa da kuma adawa a kai a kai daga manyan madafun iko. Ta bi diddigin siyasar kotu na yaudara, kishiyoyi, da zarge-zarge, gami da wadanda ke kewaye da mutuwar ’ya’yanta, don tabbatar da matsayinta.

sourceText: Through shrewd political maneuvering, strategic alliances, and a ruthless suppression of dissent, Wu Zetian consolidated power. She promoted capable officials regardless of their background, reformed the examination system, and implemented policies that strengthened the empire, ultimately proving her capability to rule as emperor.
translatedText: Wu Zetian ta hanyar dabarar siyasa mai wayo, kawancen dabaru, da murkushe masu adawa da rashin tausayi, Wu Zetian ya karfafa ikonsa. Ta kara wa manyan jami’ai karin girma ba tare da la’akari da asalinsu ba, ta gyara tsarin jarrabawa, da aiwatar da tsare-tsaren da suka karfafa daular, daga karshe ta tabbatar da iya mulkinta a matsayin sarki.

sourceText: Suffered greatly in his academic pursuits and writings due to political persecution and exile. He witnessed the sacrifice of many colleagues and disciples during the Gihae and Shinyu Persecutions.
translatedText: Ya sha wahala matuka a harkar neman ilimi da rubuce-rubucensa saboda zaluncin siyasa da gudun hijira. Ya shaida sadaukarwar abokan aiki da almajirai da yawa a lokacin Gihae da zaluncin Shinyu.

sourceText: Despite imprisonment and exile in Gangjin, he never ceased his scholarly research. Instead, he contemplated more deeply amidst hardship, writing his seminal works such as 'Mokminsimseo', 'Heumheumsinseo', and 'Gyeongseyupyo', refining his reformist ideas for the people.
translatedText: Duk da zaman kurkuku da gudun hijira a Gangjin, bai daina binciken ilimi ba. Maimakon haka, ya yi tunani sosai a cikin wahala, yana rubuta ayyukansa na farko kamar 'Mokminsimseo', 'Heumheumsinseo', da 'Gyeongseyupyo', yana mai da ra'ayinsa na kawo gyara ga mutane.

sourceText: Despite traveling to various states to promote Confucius's teachings, he was unable to realize his ideal governance due to the chaotic times. He also faced criticism and misunderstanding from those who did not follow his philosophy.
translatedText: Duk da tafiye-tafiye zuwa jihohi daban-daban don inganta koyarwar Confucius, ya kasa gane kyakkyawan shugabancinsa saboda lokacin rudani. Ya kuma fuskanci suka da rashin fahimta daga wadanda ba su bi falsafar sa ba.

sourceText: Despite setbacks, Mencius never wavered in his convictions. He taught his disciples and authored the 'Mencius,' striving to pass on his philosophy to future generations. His persistent efforts ensured his thought endures to this day.
translatedText: Duk da koma baya, Mencius bai yi kasala ba a cikin hukuncin da ya yanke. Ya koyar da almajiransa kuma ya rubuta ‘Mencius,’ yana ƙoƙarin isar da falsafarsa ga tsararraki masu zuwa. Yunkurin da ya yi ya tabbatar da tunaninsa ya dore har yau.

sourceText: He was subjected to castration (taizhi) for opposing an imperial decision, a severe punishment causing immense personal shame and the despair of being unable to continue his lineage.
translatedText: An yi masa wulakanci (taizhi) saboda adawa da shawarar da sarki ya yanke, hukunci mai tsanani yana haifar da kunya mai yawa da kuma yanke kauna na kasa ci gaba da zuriyarsa.

sourceText: Despite the humiliation and suffering, he persevered, driven by the sole purpose of completing the 'Shiji'. He transformed his disgrace into a powerful motivation, pouring all his energy into documenting historical truth and leaving behind an immortal masterpiece.
translatedText: Duk da wulakanci da wahala, ya jajirce, bisa manufar kammala 'Shiji' kawai. Ya canza wulakancinsa zuwa wani dalili mai ƙarfi, ya ba da dukkan ƙarfinsa don rubuta gaskiya ta tarihi da kuma barin babban abin da ba zai mutu ba.

sourceText: 145 BC – 86 BC
translatedText: 145 BC - 86 BC

sourceText: Zhuangzi experienced the suffering of a chaotic society and human desires by refusing official positions and living in reclusion. He deeply contemplated the pains caused by societal upheaval and the unbridled pursuit of worldly gains. He struggled to find true freedom within the constraints of reality, distancing himself from conventional values like fame and wealth.
translatedText: Zhuangzi ya fuskanci wahalar al'umma mai cike da rudani da sha'awar ɗan adam ta hanyar ƙin matsayi na hukuma da rayuwa cikin ƙima. Ya yi zurfin tunani game da radadin da ke haifar da tashin hankali na al'umma da kuma neman abin duniya mara karewa. Ya yi ƙoƙari ya sami 'yanci na gaskiya a cikin maƙasudin gaskiya, yana nisantar da kansa daga dabi'u na al'ada kamar suna da dukiya.

sourceText: Zhuangzi overcame suffering by pursuing a free spirit that transcended worldly fame and profit. He achieved inner peace by conforming to the natural flow of things, letting go of distinctions, and realizing the fundamental unity of all existence. His philosophy offers spiritual liberation beyond the limitations of the mundane world.
translatedText: Zhuangzi ya shawo kan wahala ta hanyar neman ruhu mai 'yanci wanda ya wuce shahara da riba na duniya. Ya sami kwanciyar hankali ta hanyar bin tsarin al'amura, da barin banbance-banbance, da fahimtar ainihin haɗin kai na kowane abu. Falsafarsa tana ba da 'yanci na ruhaniya fiye da iyakokin duniya.

sourceText: Zhuge Liang
translatedText: Zhuge Liang

sourceText: The precarious foundation of Shu Han, the exhaustion from constant warfare, and the immense responsibility of guiding a young emperor.
translatedText: Mummunan tushe na Shu Han, gajiyar yaƙe-yaƙe na yau da kullun, da kuma babban alhakin jagorantar wani sarki matashi.

sourceText: He overcame crises with exceptional strategy and loyalty, ensuring Shu Han's continued existence for a considerable period through his efforts.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan rikice-rikice tare da dabaru na musamman da aminci, yana tabbatar da ci gaba da wanzuwar Shu Han na wani ɗan lokaci ta hanyar ƙoƙarinsa.

sourceText: All men are my children.
translatedText: Duk maza ’ya’yana ne.

sourceText: Initially, Ashoka waged brutal wars for territorial expansion. The Kalinga War, in particular, resulted in immense loss of life and suffering, leading to deep remorse and existential questioning.
translatedText: Da farko, Ashoka ya yi munanan yaƙe-yaƙe don faɗaɗa yanki. Yaƙin Kalinga, musamman, ya haifar da hasarar rayuka da wahala, wanda ya haifar da nadama mai zurfi da tambayar wanzuwar.

sourceText: The horrors of the Kalinga War profoundly impacted Ashoka. He embraced Buddhism, dedicating himself to the principles of Dharma (righteousness, virtue, law). He then sought to rule with compassion, non-violence, and tolerance, promoting social welfare and religious harmony.
translatedText: Ta'addancin Yaƙin Kalinga ya yi tasiri sosai ga Ashoka. Ya rungumi addinin Buddha, yana sadaukar da kansa ga ka'idodin Dharma (adalci, nagarta, doka). Sannan ya nemi yin mulki cikin tausayi, rashin tashin hankali, da hakuri, da kyautata jin dadin al’umma da zamantakewar addini.

sourceText: He witnessed the tragic death of his father, Crown Prince Sado, who was confined in a rice chest by King Yeongjo. Even after ascending the throne, he suffered from extreme factional strife and threats from opposing forces. He faced many difficulties in strengthening royal power and pursuing reforms.
translatedText: Ya shaida mummunan mutuwar mahaifinsa, Yarima mai jiran gado Sado, wanda Sarki Yeongjo ya tsare a kirjin shinkafa. Ko da ya hau kan karagar mulki, ya sha fama da matsananciyar rigingimu na bangaranci da barazana daga dakarun da ke adawa da shi. Ya fuskanci matsaloli da dama wajen karfafa ikon sarauta da neman kawo sauyi.

sourceText: Never forgetting his father's tragedy, he strived to unify political factions through his policy of factional harmony and strengthening royal authority. He realized his political ideals by building Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, and fostered scholarship by establishing the Kyujanggak, thereby securing the legitimacy of his rule and overcoming threats.
translatedText: Ba tare da mantawa da bala'in mahaifinsa ba, ya yi ƙoƙari ya haɗa ɓangarorin siyasa ta hanyar manufofinsa na haɗin kai da ƙarfafa ikon sarauta. Ya tabbatar da manufofinsa na siyasa ta hanyar gina sansanin Suwon Hwaseong, kuma ya ba da guraben karatu ta hanyar kafa Kyujanggak, ta haka ne ya tabbatar da sahihancin mulkinsa da shawo kan barazanar.

sourceText: 18th Century Giant
translatedText: Giant na Karni na 18

sourceText: His aspirations for an official career were repeatedly thwarted, and he experienced significant disillusionment during turbulent political times. The gap between his ideals and reality caused him great distress.
translatedText: Burinsa na neman aiki ya ci tura akai-akai, kuma ya sha wahala sosai a lokutan siyasa da ake fama da rikici. Tazarar da ke tsakanin manufofinsa da gaskiyarsa ta haifar masa da tsananin damuwa.

sourceText: As a free spirit, he found solace in nature and wine rather than being constrained by official duties. His poetry gained depth and richness from these experiences, transforming his struggles into art.
translatedText: A matsayinsa na ruhu mai 'yanci, ya sami kwanciyar hankali a yanayi da ruwan inabi maimakon a takura masa da ayyukan hukuma. Waƙarsa ta sami zurfi da wadata daga waɗannan abubuwan da suka faru, ta mai da gwagwarmayarsa zuwa fasaha.

sourceText: Tang Dynasty (701–762)
translatedText: Daular Tang (701-762)

sourceText: Miyamoto Musashi
translatedText: Miyamoto Musashi

sourceText: From his youth, Musashi faced constant duels and threats to his life, enduring social isolation as he continuously honed himself. Living through an era of war and turmoil, he witnessed and experienced countless deaths.
translatedText: Tun daga ƙuruciyarsa, Musashi yana fuskantar tashe-tashen hankula da barazana ga rayuwarsa, tare da jure wa kaɗaici yayin da ya ci gaba da girmama kansa. Lokacin da yake rayuwa cikin zamanin yaƙi da hargitsi, ya shaida kuma ya fuskanci mace-mace marasa adadi.

sourceText: Through rigorous training, meditation, and communion with nature, he found inner peace. By systematizing his unique sword techniques and authoring 'The Book of Five Rings,' he chose to pass on not only the wisdom of combat but also life's teachings to future generations.
translatedText: Ta hanyar horo mai tsauri, tunani, da tarayya da yanayi, ya sami kwanciyar hankali. Ta hanyar tsara dabarun takobinsa na musamman da marubucin 'Littafin Zobba Biyar,' ya zaɓi ba da hikimar yaƙi kaɗai ba har ma da koyarwar rayuwa ga tsararraki masu zuwa.

sourceText: Oda Nobunaga
translatedText: Oda Nobunaga

sourceText: Initially mocked as the 'Fool of Owari' for his eccentricities, Nobunaga faced constant betrayals from within his own ranks and the relentless struggles of endless warfare.
translatedText: Da farko an yi masa ba'a a matsayin 'Wawa na Owari' saboda ƙazaminsa, Nobunaga ya fuskanci cin amana akai-akai daga cikin sahunsa da kuma gwagwarmayar yaƙi mara iyaka.

sourceText: Unswayed by conventional wisdom or the counsel of hesitant retainers, he boldly adopted innovative tactics and weaponry, notably firearms, to overcome the chaotic era and lay the groundwork for Japan's unification.
translatedText: Ba tare da hikima ta al'ada ba ko shawarar masu riƙe da riƙon ƙwarya, da ƙarfin hali ya ɗauki sabbin dabaru da makami, musamman bindigogi, don shawo kan rikice-rikicen zamanin tare da aza harsashin haɗin kan Japan.

sourceText: Sun Yat-sen faced immense challenges, including repeated failed uprisings against the corrupt Qing Dynasty, exile, and the constant threat of imperialist powers seeking to partition China. Internal divisions among revolutionaries and the rise of warlords also presented significant obstacles.
translatedText: Sun Yat-sen ya fuskanci kalubale masu tarin yawa, ciki har da tada kayar baya da aka yi a baya-bayan nan a kan gurbatattun daular Qing, gudun hijira, da kuma barazanar da masu mulkin mallaka ke yi a kai a kai da ke neman raba kasar Sin. rarrabuwar kawuna tsakanin 'yan juyin-juya-hali da tashin gwanayen yaki su ma sun kawo cikas.

sourceText: He persevered through unwavering dedication to his vision of a modern, independent China, articulated in his Three Principles of the People. By tirelessly organizing, educating, and inspiring followers, he galvanized the revolutionary movement, culminating in the successful Xinhai Revolution and the establishment of the Republic of China.
translatedText: Ya jajirce ta hanyar sadaukar da kai ga hangen nesansa na kasar Sin ta zamani, mai cin gashin kanta, wanda ya bayyana a cikin ka'idojinsa guda uku na jama'a. Ta hanyar tsara tsari, da ilmantar da mabiya, da karfafa guiwa, ya karfafa yunkurin juyin juya hali, wanda ya kai ga nasarar juyin juya halin Xinhai, da kafa jamhuriyar Sin.

sourceText: Tokugawa Ieyasu
translatedText: Tokugawa Ieyasu

sourceText: As a child, I was held hostage due to the conflict between the Oda and Imagawa clans, leading a precarious existence. Throughout my life, I faced constant threats to my survival, navigating numerous alliances, betrayals, and battles.
translatedText: Tun ina karama, an yi garkuwa da ni saboda rikicin da ya barke tsakanin kabilar Oda da Imagawa, wanda ya haifar da mummunan rayuwa. A tsawon rayuwata, na fuskanci barazana ga rayuwata a kai a kai, na shiga ƙawance da yawa, cin amana, da kuma yaƙe-yaƙe.

sourceText: I overcame these adversies with unwavering patience and a cool, strategic mind, refusing to yield regardless of the circumstances. I forged alliances with a long-term vision and made bold decisions at critical junctures to secure victory.
translatedText: Na shawo kan waɗannan masifu tare da haƙuri mara kaushi da sanyin hankali, dabarar tunani, na ƙi ba da kyauta ba tare da la'akari da yanayin ba. Na kulla kawance tare da hangen nesa na dogon lokaci kuma na yanke shawarar yanke shawara a lokuta masu mahimmanci don samun nasara.

sourceText: Toyotomi Hideyoshi
translatedText: Toyota Hideyoshi

sourceText: If you have a plan that doesn't work, then the plan is flawed.
translatedText: Idan kuna da tsarin da ba ya aiki, to shirin yana da lahani.

sourceText: Born into poverty as a farmer's son, social mobility was extremely difficult in his era. He also faced life-or-death struggles on numerous battlefields.
translatedText: An haife shi cikin talauci a matsayin ɗan manomi, motsin jama'a yana da matukar wahala a zamaninsa. Ya kuma fuskanci gwagwarmayar rayuwa ko kisa a fagagen yaki da dama.

sourceText: He gained the trust of Oda Nobunaga, leading to rapid advancement. Through his exceptional strategies and political acumen, he pacified the warring states and unified Japan.
translatedText: Ya sami amincewar Oda Nobunaga, wanda ke haifar da ci gaba cikin sauri. Ta hanyar dabarunsa na musamman da basirar siyasa, ya daidaita jihohin da ke rikici tare da hada kan Japan.

sourceText: Chulalongkorn
translatedText: Chulalongkorn

sourceText: Upon ascending the throne, Chulalongkorn faced the dual challenges of a feudalistic, backward societal structure and the looming threat of foreign powers. Maintaining Siam's independence amidst the imperialistic ambitions of Western nations was a constant diplomatic and political struggle. He also had to contend with internal conservative factions resistant to reform.
translatedText: Lokacin da ya hau kan karagar mulki, Chulalongkorn ya fuskanci kalubale biyu na tsarin zamantakewar al'umma, koma baya da kuma barazanar da kasashen ketare ke fuskanta. Tsayawa Siam 'yancin kai a cikin muradin mulkin mallaka na kasashen yammacin Turai ya kasance gwagwarmayar diflomasiyya da siyasa akai-akai. Har ila yau, dole ne ya yi gwagwarmaya da ƙungiyoyin masu ra'ayin mazan jiya na cikin gida masu adawa da gyara.

sourceText: Chulalongkorn navigated these challenges with astute diplomacy and bold reform policies. He personally visited Europe to learn from Western advancements and modernized Siam's legal, administrative, and educational systems. Notably, he gradually abolished slavery, reformed the tax system, and established a centralized administration, fostering stability and progress. These efforts ensured Siam remained the only Southeast Asian nation to avoid colonization.
translatedText: Chulalongkorn ya zagaya wadannan kalubaloli tare da diflomasiyya mai hazaka da jajircewar manufofin kawo sauyi. Shi da kansa ya ziyarci Turai don koyo daga ci gaban yammacin Turai da sabunta tsarin shari'a, gudanarwa, da ilimi na Siam. Musamman ma, a hankali ya kawar da bautar, ya gyara tsarin haraji, ya kafa gwamnati mai zaman kanta, ta samar da kwanciyar hankali da ci gaba. Wannan ƙoƙarin ya tabbatar da cewa Siam ta kasance ƙasa ɗaya tilo ta kudu maso gabashin Asiya don gujewa mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: The early death of his father Hamilcar, the lifelong burden of vengeance against Rome, and the isolation and sacrifices that came with constant warfare.
translatedText: Farkon mutuwar mahaifinsa Hamilcar, nauyin ɗaukar fansa a kan Roma, da keɓewa da sadaukarwa waɗanda suka zo tare da yaƙe-yaƙe.

sourceText: Achieved numerous victories through exceptional military talent and an indomitable will, overcoming hardships by fostering deep bonds with his soldiers.
translatedText: Ya sami nasarori masu yawa ta hanyar hazaka na soja na musamman da kuma iƙirarin da ba zai iya jurewa ba, shawo kan wahalhalu ta hanyar haɓaka zurfafa dangantaka da sojojinsa.

sourceText: 3rd - 2nd Century BC
translatedText: Karni na 3-2 BC

sourceText: Ibn Battuta
translatedText: Ibn Battuta

sourceText: Ibn Battuta faced numerous dangers and adversies. He encountered pirate attacks, threats from bandits, illnesses, political instability, and the barriers of unfamiliar cultures and languages. He experienced moments of separation from companions, loss of possessions, and even threats to his life.
translatedText: Ibn Battuta ya fuskanci hatsari da masifu da dama. Ya ci karo da hare-haren 'yan fashi, barazanar 'yan fashi, cututtuka, rashin kwanciyar hankali na siyasa, da shingen al'adu da harsunan da ba a sani ba. Ya fuskanci lokuta na rabuwa da sahabbai, asarar dukiya, har ma da barazana ga rayuwarsa.

sourceText: Ibn Battuta overcame these challenges through his remarkable adaptability, deep faith, and profound respect for local cultures. He navigated perilous situations with wisdom and diplomacy, made new friends, and learned to respect the customs of each region. His persistent spirit of exploration and passion for documentation drove him forward.
translatedText: Ibn Battuta ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar dacewarsa na ban mamaki, zurfin imaninsa, da matuƙar mutunta al'adun gida. Ya kewaya yanayi masu haɗari da hikima da diflomasiyya, ya sami sabbin abokai, kuma ya koyi mutunta al'adun kowane yanki. Dagewar ruhinsa na bincike da sha'awar rubuce-rubuce ya sa shi gaba.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14

sourceText: Jayavarman VII
translatedText: Jayavarman VII

sourceText: Jayavarman VII
translatedText: Jayavarman VII

sourceText: Constant warfare with the Chams, widespread epidemics, and famines that afflicted his people, leading to immense suffering. The later years of his reign also saw political instability.
translatedText: Yaƙi na yau da kullun da Chams, annoba mai yaɗuwa, da yunwa da suka addabi mutanensa, wanda ya haifar da wahala mai yawa. Bayan shekarun mulkinsa kuma an ga rashin zaman lafiya a siyasance.

sourceText: Driven by his Mahayana Buddhist compassion, he initiated massive public works, including hospitals, roads, and bridges, to improve the lives of his subjects and stabilize the realm. He also commissioned monumental architecture like Angkor Thom and the Bayon temple.
translatedText: Tausayin addinin Buddah Mahayana ne ya jagorance shi, ya ƙaddamar da manyan ayyuka na jama'a, waɗanda suka haɗa da asibitoci, hanyoyi, da gadoji, don inganta rayuwar talakawansa da daidaita daular. Ya kuma ba da umarni manyan gine-gine kamar Angkor Thom da haikalin Bayon.

sourceText: 12th - 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12-13

sourceText: Before my famous Hajj, the vast wealth and influence of the Mali Empire were largely unknown to the outside world. Internally, maintaining control over such a vast territory and ensuring its prosperity presented continuous challenges, alongside potential internal dissent.
translatedText: Kafin aikin Hajji na shahara, dimbin dukiya da tasirin daular Mali ba su da masaniya a wajen duniya. A ciki, kiyaye iko a kan irin wannan yanki mai faɗi da kuma tabbatar da wadatar sa ya gabatar da ƙalubale masu ci gaba, tare da yuwuwar rashin yarda na cikin gida.

sourceText: Through strategic expansion, the establishment of important cities like Timbuktu as centers of learning and trade, and a meticulously planned pilgrimage that showcased Mali's immense riches, I projected an image of power and prosperity. I fostered scholarship and the arts, solidifying Mali's golden age.
translatedText: Ta hanyar fadada dabaru, da kafa muhimman birane kamar Timbuktu a matsayin cibiyoyin koyo da kasuwanci, da kuma gudanar da aikin hajjin da aka tsara sosai wanda ya nuna dimbin arzikin kasar Mali, na yi hasashe na nuna karfin iko da wadata. Na haɓaka guraben karatu da fasaha, tare da ƙarfafa shekarun zinare na Mali.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14

sourceText: As an outcast in his youth, Shaka endured hardship, rejection, and constant struggle for survival alongside his mother, facing hunger and humiliation.
translatedText: A matsayinsa na wanda aka yi watsi da shi a cikin kuruciyarsa, Shaka ya jure wahala, kin amincewa, da gwagwarmayar rayuwa tare da mahaifiyarsa, yana fuskantar yunwa da wulakanci.

sourceText: These trials forged his resilience and leadership. He excelled in military training, becoming a formidable warrior, and used his political acumen to unite tribes and establish the Zulu Kingdom.
translatedText: Wadannan gwaje-gwaje sun haifar da juriya da jagoranci. Ya yi fice wajen horar da sojoji, inda ya zama jarumi, kuma ya yi amfani da basirarsa ta siyasa wajen hada kan kabilu da kafa masarautar Zulu.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 18th - Farkon Ƙarni na 19

sourceText: Mathematics is the foundation of all knowledge and the key to understanding the universe.
translatedText: Lissafi shine ginshikin dukkan ilimi kuma mabuɗin fahimtar sararin samaniya.

sourceText: Scholarly pursuits often faced religious and political constraints, as well as societal biases of the time. Accessing rare manuscripts and translating knowledge from disparate languages presented significant challenges.
translatedText: Kokarin neman ilimi sau da yawa yana fuskantar matsalolin addini da na siyasa, da kuma son zuciya na al'umma na lokacin. Samun damar rubuce-rubucen rubuce-rubucen da ba kasafai ba da fassarar ilimi daga harsuna dabam-dabam sun ba da ƙalubale masu mahimmanci.

sourceText: Through relentless curiosity and collaboration with fellow scholars, I surmounted these intellectual barriers. By ensuring my treatises were translated into Latin, I facilitated the dissemination of knowledge across Europe, fostering a new era of mathematical understanding.
translatedText: Ta hanyar son sani da haɗin gwiwa tare da ’yan uwa malamai, na tsallake waɗannan shingen hankali. Ta hanyar tabbatar da cewa an fassara littatafai na zuwa Latin, na sauƙaƙe yada ilimi a duk faɗin Turai, na haɓaka sabon zamanin fahimtar ilimin lissafi.

sourceText: 9th Century
translatedText: Karni na 9

sourceText: I measure the progress of a community by the degree of progress which women have achieved.
translatedText: Ina auna ci gaban al’umma da irin ci gaban da mata suka samu.

sourceText: He faced immense discrimination and humiliation due to the caste system, enduring numerous hardships to gain access to education. He had to fight against opposition and prejudice from the ruling class.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci tsananin wariya da wulakanci saboda tsarin kabilanci, tare da jure wahalhalu da dama don samun damar karatu. Dole ne ya yi yaƙi da hamayya da kuma ra’ayin masu mulki.

sourceText: He proved himself through outstanding academic achievements and tireless effort. He reformed the system through the power of law and politics, advocating for the rights of the marginalized and giving voice to the oppressed.
translatedText: Ya tabbatar da kansa ta hanyar fitattun nasarorin ilimi da ƙoƙari mara gajiyawa. Ya gyara tsarin ne ta hanyar doka da siyasa, yana ba da ra'ayin kare hakkin wadanda aka zalunta da ba da murya ga wadanda aka zalunta.

sourceText: Mid-20th Century
translatedText: Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: Chandragupta Maurya
translatedText: Chandragupta Maurya

sourceText: Laid the Foundation of an Empire
translatedText: Kafa Harsashin Daular

sourceText: Victory is never permanent, nor is defeat.
translatedText: Nasara ba ta dawwama, kuma ba ta dawwama.

sourceText: He faced hardships in his childhood, including the absence of his father, and had to survive constant political intrigue and warfare to seize power. Building an empire amidst the power struggles of the Magadha kingdom and the invasions of Alexander the Great was an immensely difficult undertaking.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci wahalhalu a lokacin kuruciyarsa, ciki har da rashin mahaifinsa, kuma dole ne ya tsallake rijiya da baya na siyasa da yakin neman kwace mulki. Gina daula a tsakanin gwagwarmayar ikon masarautar Magadha da mamayewar Alexander the Great aiki ne mai wuyar gaske.

sourceText: Chandragupta overcame numerous crises with exceptional strategy and determination. With the help of wise advisors like Chanakya, he honed his military and political capabilities, established a strong centralized government, and implemented an efficient administrative system, thus stabilizing the empire.
translatedText: Chandragupta ya shawo kan rikice-rikice da yawa tare da dabaru na musamman da azama. Tare da taimakon masu ba da shawara masu hikima irin su Chanakya, ya inganta aikin soja da na siyasa, ya kafa gwamnati mai karfi, ya aiwatar da tsarin gudanarwa mai inganci, ta haka ya daidaita daular.

sourceText: 4th Century BCE
translatedText: Karni na 4 KZ

sourceText: Omar Khayyam
translatedText: Umar Khayyam

sourceText: A jug of wine, a loaf of bread, and thou beside me singing in the wilderness – and wilderness is paradise enow.
translatedText: Tulun ruwan inabi, da waina, Kai kuma tare da ni kuna raira waƙa a jeji, jeji kuwa aljanna ce.

sourceText: He navigated the volatile political landscape of Seljuk Persia, facing potential persecution for his philosophical ideas and scientific inquiries. The patronage of rulers was essential but often precarious, subject to their whims and the shifting tides of power.
translatedText: Ya zagaya yanayin siyasar Seljuk Farisa, inda ya fuskanci zaluntar ra'ayoyinsa na falsafa da binciken kimiyya. Taimakon masu mulki ya kasance mai mahimmanci amma sau da yawa yana da haɗari, bisa ga son rai da sauye-sauye na iko.

sourceText: Securing patronage allowed him to establish and work at the Nishapur observatory, contributing significantly to astronomical calculations and calendar reform. His poetry, though sometimes controversial, provided solace and a philosophical outlet, ensuring his legacy transcended his turbulent times.
translatedText: Tabbatar da ikon mallakar ya ba shi damar kafawa da aiki a Nishapur Observatory, yana ba da gudummawa sosai ga lissafin taurari da sake fasalin kalanda. Waƙarsa, ko da yake wani lokaci tana da rigima, ta ba da ta'aziyya da hanyar falsafa, ta tabbatar da gadonsa ya zarce lokacinsa na tashin hankali.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11-12

sourceText: Robert Oppenheimer
translatedText: Robert Oppenheimer ne adam wata

sourceText: Now I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds.
translatedText: Yanzu na zama Mutuwa, mai halakar talikai.

sourceText: He experienced profound guilt and ethical anguish over the use of nuclear weapons, which caused immense loss of life, as the head of the atomic bomb's development. Later, during the McCarthy era, he faced political persecution, being stripped of his security clearance and removed from his advisory role in nuclear development due to suspicions of communism.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci babban laifi da bacin rai game da amfani da makaman nukiliya, wanda ya haifar da hasarar rayuka, a matsayinsa na shugaban ci gaban bam ɗin. Daga baya, a lokacin McCarthy, ya fuskanci zalunci na siyasa, an cire shi daga izinin tsaro da kuma cire shi daga matsayinsa na ba da shawara a ci gaban nukiliya saboda zato na gurguzu.

sourceText: He deeply acknowledged the moral responsibility for his actions and dedicated himself to advocating for nuclear arms control and the peaceful application of atomic energy. He strove to ensure scientific progress benefited humanity, steering his intellectual legacy in a positive direction.
translatedText: Ya yarda da haƙƙin ɗabi'a na ayyukansa kuma ya sadaukar da kansa don ba da shawarar sarrafa makaman nukiliya da amfani da makamashin nukiliya cikin lumana. Ya yi ƙoƙari don tabbatar da ci gaban kimiyya ya amfanar ɗan adam, yana jagorantar gadonsa na hankali a hanya mai kyau.

sourceText: Mid-20th Century
translatedText: Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: Chivalry is not part of the battle.
translatedText: Chivalry ba ya cikin yaƙin.

sourceText: He experienced an unstable childhood moving between Syria and Iraq, and suffered the tragedy of his brother's death. He also endured continuous wars with the Crusaders, leading to many sacrifices.
translatedText: Ya gamu da rashin kwanciyar hankali yana tafiya tsakanin Siriya da Iraki, kuma ya sha wahalar mutuwar dan uwansa. Ya kuma jimre ci gaba da yaƙe-yaƙe da 'yan Salibiyya, wanda ya kai ga sadaukarwa da yawa.

sourceText: After his brother's death, he felt a stronger sense of responsibility and utilized his military talents to unite the Islamic world and defeat the Crusaders. His forgiveness and magnanimity earned him respect even from his enemies.
translatedText: Bayan rasuwar dan uwansa, sai ya ji wani irin nauyi da ya rataya a wuyansa, ya kuma yi amfani da kwarewarsa ta soja wajen hada kan kasashen musulmi da kuma fatattakar 'yan Salibiyya. Gafararsa da girmansa ya sa ake girmama shi hatta a wajen makiyansa.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12

sourceText: Srinivasa Ramanujan
translatedText: Srinivasa Ramanujan

sourceText: An equation for me has no meaning unless it expresses a thought of God.
translatedText: Ma'auni a gare ni ba shi da ma'ana sai dai ya bayyana tunanin Allah.

sourceText: Poverty, illness, and lack of formal academic recognition.
translatedText: Talauci, rashin lafiya, da rashin sanin ilimin boko.

sourceText: Perseverance fueled by an insatiable passion for mathematics, and crucial support from G.H. Hardy.
translatedText: Juriya ta haifar da sha'awar ilimin lissafi, da mahimmancin tallafi daga G.H. Hardy.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Revenge is not the act of a warrior, but of a man who has lost his honor.
translatedText: Ɗaukar fansa ba aikin jarumi ba ne, amma na mutumin da ya rasa darajarsa.

sourceText: Suleiman faced profound personal tragedies, including the intense rivalry and conflict among his sons for succession, culminating in the execution of his eldest son, Mustafa. These events deeply affected him.
translatedText: Suleiman ya fuskanci bala’o’i masu yawa, wadanda suka hada da tsananin gaba da rikici tsakanin ‘ya’yansa maza da suka gaje shi, har ya kai ga kashe babban dansa Mustafa. Waɗannan abubuwan sun shafe shi sosai.

sourceText: Despite these personal sorrows, Suleiman remained dedicated to the welfare and stability of his vast empire. He focused on strengthening his administration, codifying laws, patronizing arts and sciences, and continuing military campaigns, ensuring the empire's continued prosperity and influence.
translatedText: Duk da wannan bakin ciki na kansa, Suleiman ya kasance mai sadaukarwa ga jin dadi da kwanciyar hankali na daularsa. Ya mayar da hankali kan karfafa gwamnatinsa, tsara dokoki, tallafawa fasaha da kimiyya, da ci gaba da yakin soja, tabbatar da ci gaba da wadata da tasirin daular.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karni na 16

sourceText: Swami Vivekananda
translatedText: Swami Vivekananda

sourceText: Arise, awake, and stop not till the goal is reached.
translatedText: Tashi, farka, kuma tsaya har sai an cimma burin.

sourceText: From his youth, he faced familial poverty and the immense responsibility of providing for his family after his father's sudden death. He also battled societal prejudice and skepticism towards his unconventional spiritual path. Throughout his spiritual journey, he contended with material hardships and profound inner conflicts.
translatedText: Tun daga ƙuruciyarsa, ya fuskanci talauci na iyali da kuma babban nauyin kula da iyalinsa bayan mutuwar mahaifinsa ba zato ba tsammani. Ya kuma yi yaƙi da son zuciya na al'umma da shakku game da tafarkinsa na ruhaniya wanda ba na al'ada ba. A cikin tafiyarsa ta ruhaniya, ya yi fama da wahalhalu na abin duniya da manyan rikice-rikice na ciki.

sourceText: Through his profound encounter with his guru, Ramakrishna, he attained spiritual enlightenment, which enabled him to transcend his personal suffering. With persistent meditation, rigorous spiritual practice, and an unwavering conviction in his mission, he overcame poverty and societal pressures to become a global ambassador for Indian spirituality.
translatedText: Ta hanyar gamuwar da ya yi da guru, Ramakrishna, ya sami wayewar ruhaniya, wanda ya ba shi damar ƙetare wahalarsa. Tare da ci gaba da zurfafa tunani, tsauraran ayyuka na ruhaniya, da kuma tabbatarwa a cikin manufarsa, ya shawo kan talauci da matsalolin al'umma ya zama jakadan duniya na ruhaniya na Indiya.

sourceText: Late 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi karni na 19

sourceText: Wright Brothers
translatedText: Wright Brothers

sourceText: We were convinced that if men could be made to fly, it would be a matter of a few years only.
translatedText: Muna da yakinin cewa idan za a iya sanya maza su tashi, zai zama batun na ’yan shekaru ne kawai.

sourceText: Lack of funding, technical challenges, skepticism about powered flight, repeated failures and accidents.
translatedText: Rashin kuɗi, ƙalubalen fasaha, shakku game da jirgin sama mai ƙarfi, sake lalacewa da haɗari.

sourceText: Persistent experimentation and research, deep understanding of flight principles, unwavering belief in their technology, mutual encouragement and collaboration.
translatedText: Gwajin gwaji da bincike mai tsayi, zurfin fahimtar ka'idodin jirgin, imani maras tabbas game da fasahar su, ƙarfafa juna da haɗin gwiwa.

sourceText: Late 19th Century ~ Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi Karni na 19 ~ Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Smith suffered from a noticeable stammer from a young age, which presented challenges in his social interactions and public speaking. Furthermore, his economic theories often clashed with the prevailing mercantilist policies of his time, drawing significant criticism.
translatedText: Smith ya sha wahala daga ƙwanƙwasa da aka sani tun yana ƙarami, wanda ya gabatar da ƙalubale a cikin mu'amalar sa da jama'a. Bugu da ƙari, ka'idodin tattalin arzikinsa sau da yawa suna cin karo da manufofin 'yan kasuwa na zamaninsa, suna jawo zargi mai mahimmanci.

sourceText: Smith overcame these difficulties through persistent writing, deep contemplation, and the continuous development of his ideas. Despite his speech impediment, he earned recognition for his intellectual prowess through his university lectures. His published works gradually gained traction, influencing academia and society, and disseminating his philosophies widely.
translatedText: Smith ya shawo kan waɗannan matsalolin ta hanyar dagewar rubuce-rubuce, zurfafa tunani, da ci gaba da haɓaka ra'ayoyinsa. Duk da rashin iya magana, ya samu karramawa saboda bajintar da yake da shi ta hanyar karatun jami'a. Ayyukansa da aka buga a hankali sun sami karɓuwa, suna yin tasiri ga ilimi da al'umma, da yada falsafancinsa a ko'ina.

sourceText: Enlightenment
translatedText: Fadakarwa

sourceText: Give me a place to stand, and I shall move the Earth.
translatedText: Ka ba ni wuri in tsaya, in motsa duniya.

sourceText: The instability of life amidst political turmoil and war, and the anguish over his inventions potentially being used for destructive purposes.
translatedText: Rashin kwanciyar hankali na rayuwa a cikin rudanin siyasa da yaƙe-yaƙe, da bacin rai a kan abubuwan da ya ƙirƙira da za a iya amfani da su don dalilai masu ɓarna.

sourceText: By immersing himself in intellectual inquiry and scientific discovery, he maintained an unshaken inner peace amidst chaotic reality, creating knowledge that would endure for humanity.
translatedText: Ta hanyar nutsar da kansa cikin bincike na hankali da binciken kimiyya, ya kiyaye zaman lafiya na ciki mara girgiza a cikin haƙiƙanin ruɗani, ƙirƙirar ilimin da zai dawwama ga ɗan adam.

sourceText: Hellenistic period
translatedText: Lokacin Hellenistic

sourceText: The fear of publishing his theories, which contradicted the dominant geocentric model, and the potential social and religious backlash. Also, the long time it took to complete and publish his research.
translatedText: Tsoron wallafa ra'ayoyinsa, wanda ya saba wa tsarin tsarin geocentric mai rinjaye, da kuma yuwuwar koma bayan zamantakewa da addini. Har ila yau, tsawon lokacin da aka dauka don kammalawa da buga bincikensa.

sourceText: Confident in his observations and calculations, he published 'De revolutionibus orbium coelestium' only as he approached death. This was the result of his lifelong research and courage.
translatedText: Da yake da kwarin guiwa a cikin abubuwan da ya lura da kuma lissafinsa, ya buga 'De revolutionibus orbium coelesium' kawai lokacin da ya kusa mutuwa. Wannan shi ne sakamakon bincikensa da jajircewarsa na tsawon rayuwarsa.

sourceText: Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance

sourceText: Dante Alighieri
translatedText: Dante Alighieri

sourceText: Love, that moves the sun and the other stars.
translatedText: Ƙauna, wannan yana motsa rana da sauran taurari.

sourceText: Dante faced profound personal and political turmoil, including exile from his beloved Florence, the premature death of his muse Beatrice, and constant political strife. This exile stripped him of his civic life and homeland.
translatedText: Dante ya fuskanci rikice-rikice na sirri da na siyasa, ciki har da gudun hijira daga ƙaunatacciyarsa Florence, mutuwar gidan tarihinsa na Beatrice, da kuma rikice-rikice na siyasa. Wannan gudun hijira ya kore shi daga rayuwar al'ummarsa da kasarsa.

sourceText: He channelled his suffering and profound spiritual journey into the 'Divine Comedy.' This epic poem, a synthesis of medieval thought, theology, and his personal experiences, became his means of processing his pain, seeking divine justice, and leaving an indelible mark on literature and Western thought.
translatedText: Ya ba da wahalarsa da tafiya mai zurfi ta ruhaniya cikin 'Divine Comedy'. Wannan waka ta almara, haɗe-haɗe na tunani na zamanin da, tiyoloji, da abubuwan da ya same shi, ta zama hanyarsa ta sarrafa zafinsa, da neman adalcin Ubangiji, da barin tabo maras gogewa a kan adabi da tunanin Turawa.

sourceText: Middle Ages
translatedText: Tsakanin Zamani

sourceText: Johannes Gutenberg
translatedText: Johannes Gutenberg

sourceText: Gutenberg faced immense financial challenges to fund his invention and suffered greatly from a lawsuit with his business partner, Johann Fust, which led to his bankruptcy and the seizure of his printing equipment.
translatedText: Gutenberg ya fuskanci kalubalen kudi masu tarin yawa don samar da kudaden da ya kirkiro kuma ya sha wahala sosai daga karar da abokin kasuwancinsa, Johann Fust, ya kai ga fatarar sa da kuma kwace kayan aikin bugawa.

sourceText: Despite severe financial hardship and legal battles, he persevered, continuing his printing work on a smaller scale. While his invention's full impact wasn't realized in his lifetime, it laid the foundation for future advancements in printing technology.
translatedText: Duk da matsananciyar wahala na kuɗi da yaƙin shari’a, ya jajirce, ya ci gaba da aikin buga littattafai a ƙanƙanta. Duk da yake ba a sami cikakken tasirin abin da ya ƙirƙira a rayuwarsa ba, ya kafa harsashin ci gaba a nan gaba a fasahar bugawa.

sourceText: Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance

sourceText: Louis Pasteur
translatedText: Louis Pasteur

sourceText: Pasteur faced significant opposition from the established scientific community, particularly regarding his germ theory of disease, which challenged the prevailing spontaneous generation theory. He also endured personal tragedy, including the loss of his daughter to illness.
translatedText: Pasteur ya fuskanci adawa mai yawa daga kafaffen al'ummar kimiyya, musamman game da ka'idarsa ta kwayoyin cuta, wacce ta kalubalanci ka'idar tsararraki da ke gudana. Ya kuma jure wa wani bala'i na kansa, ciki har da rashin 'yarsa da rashin lafiya.

sourceText: Through meticulous experimentation and irrefutable evidence, Pasteur systematically disproved spontaneous generation and validated germ theory. His development of vaccines for anthrax, rabies, and other diseases, alongside the introduction of pasteurization, revolutionized medicine and public health, solidifying his legacy.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ƙwaƙƙwaran gwaji da shaidar da ba za ta iya murmurewa ba, Pasteur a tsanake ya karyata tsarar da ba ta dace ba da ingantaccen ka'idar ƙwayoyin cuta. Haɓakarsa na rigakafin cutar anthrax, rabies, da sauran cututtuka, tare da ƙaddamar da kiwo, juyin juya halin likita da lafiyar jama'a, yana ƙarfafa gadonsa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Machiavelli
translatedText: Machiavelli

sourceText: He was exiled from public office following the return of the Medici family, subjected to torture, and witnessed the failure of his political ideals.
translatedText: An kore shi daga ofishin gwamnati bayan dawowar dangin Medici, an azabtar da shi, kuma ya shaida gazawar manufofinsa na siyasa.

sourceText: During his painful exile, he continued to write seminal works such as 'The Prince' and 'Discourses on Livy,' reflecting deeply on political philosophy. This was a testament to his enduring intellectual spirit and political experience.
translatedText: A lokacin gudun hijira mai raɗaɗi, ya ci gaba da rubuta ayyukan ilimi kamar su 'The Prince' da 'Magana akan Livy,' suna tunani sosai kan falsafar siyasa. Wannan ya kasance shaida ga ruhinsa na hazaka da gogewar siyasa.

sourceText: Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance

sourceText: I disapprove of what you say, but I will defend to the death your right to say it.
translatedText: Ban yarda da abin da kuke fada ba, amma zan kare hakkin ku na fada har mutuwa.

sourceText: Voltaire endured lifelong struggles against religious intolerance, censorship, and political oppression. He was imprisoned twice and exiled from France, with his writings often banned.
translatedText: Voltaire ya jimre gwagwarmayar rayuwa na tsawon rai da rashin haƙuri na addini, ba da izini, da zalunci na siyasa. An daure shi sau biyu kuma aka kore shi daga Faransa, tare da hana rubuce-rubucensa sau da yawa.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges through his prolific writing and skillful diplomacy. By exposing injustice and advocating for freedom and tolerance, he confronted those in power. His persistent efforts significantly advanced the ideals of the Enlightenment.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar ƙwararrun rubuce-rubucensa da ƙwararrun diflomasiyya. Ta hanyar fallasa zalunci da ba da ra'ayin 'yanci da hakuri, ya fuskanci masu mulki. Ƙoƙarinsa na ci gaba da inganta manufofin wayewar kai.

sourceText: Age of Enlightenment
translatedText: Zamanin Fadakarwa

sourceText: Alexander Graham Bell
translatedText: Alexander Graham Bell

sourceText: His personal experience with hearing loss and his deep involvement in educating the deaf profoundly shaped his work. He also faced numerous failed experiments and financial struggles.
translatedText: Kwarewarsa ta sirri game da rashin ji da zurfin shigarsa wajen ilimantar da kurame sun tsara aikin nasa sosai. Ya kuma fuskanci gwaje-gwajen da ba su yi nasara ba da kuma gwagwarmayar kuɗi.

sourceText: Bell's relentless experimentation and perseverance were key to overcoming these challenges. His insights gained from working with the deaf provided crucial motivation for his inventions.
translatedText: Gwajin gwajin da tsayin daka na Bell ya yi shine mabuɗin don shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubale. Abubuwan da ya samu daga aiki tare da kurame sun ba da kwarin gwiwa mai mahimmanci ga abubuwan da ya kirkira.

sourceText: 19th-20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19-20

sourceText: Amelia Earhart
translatedText: Amelia Earhart ne adam wata

sourceText: As a woman in a male-dominated field, Earhart faced significant prejudice and skepticism, making it difficult to secure funding and recognition for her aviation endeavors. The constant glare of public attention and media scrutiny also presented personal challenges.
translatedText: A matsayinta na mace a fagen da maza ke mamaye, Earhart ta fuskanci babban ra'ayi da shakku, wanda hakan ya sa ya zama da wahala a sami tallafi da kuma karramawa ga ayyukanta na jirgin sama. Haskakawa da jama'a akai-akai da kuma binciken kafofin watsa labarai sun gabatar da kalubale na sirri.

sourceText: Earhart overcame these obstacles through sheer determination, skill, and a refusal to be limited by societal expectations. She actively promoted aviation for women, breaking barriers and serving as a role model. Her writing and public speaking further amplified her message of empowerment.
translatedText: Earhart ya shawo kan waɗannan cikas ta hanyar azama, fasaha, da ƙin iyakancewa da tsammanin al'umma. Ta himmatu wajen inganta harkokin sufurin jiragen sama ga mata, ta karya shinge da kuma zama abin koyi. Rubuce-rubucen da ta yi da kuma yin magana da jama'a sun ƙara ƙarfafa sakonta na ƙarfafawa.
